exemplar.title: An Investigation Into Optimal Caffeine Intake

detailedExemplarData.title: An Investigation Into Optimal Caffeine Intake

subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Investigation follows a clear sequence (introduction â model construction â analysis â conclusion)
Sections are labelled and include tables, figures, and headings providing adequate structure
Most content directly relates to the stated aim of modelling caffeine alertness
weaknesses: Transitions between sections can be abrupt and minor gaps interrupt flow (e.g., unfinished sentence on page 2)
Figure and table captions are often separated from their objects, disrupting readability
Some background repetition and lengthy quotations reduce conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is coherent with logical development evident in the overall sequence, and the structure is adequate with labelled sections and supporting material. However, abrupt transitions, misplaced captions, and occasional irrelevance prevent it from reaching the higher bands. These observations align with a Band 2 overall performance in Presentation.

strengths: Correct use of standard notation for N(t), half-life modelling, and slope formula in several places
Inclusion of multiple representations (tables, GeoGebra graphs, piecewise equations)
Calculations and accompanying explanations are generally clear and follow a logical progression
weaknesses: Variables are re-defined without clarification and units are sometimes omitted
Rounding inconsistencies (e.g., 0.44 vs 0.444) and typographical slips reduce precision
Some axes and plots lack labels or legends, weakening the link between representations and narrative
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language and notation are mostly appropriate, and the submission employs several representations. Communication is generally clear, but inconsistencies in notation, missing units, and inadequate linking of representations limit the work to Band 2 in Mathematical Communication.

strengths: The choice of topic is clearly motivated by the studentâs personal IB experience, showing independent thinking
A clear personal approaches drives modelling decisions and choice of parameters
The investigation demonstrates significant evidence of self-driven model construction
weaknesses: The mathematical techniques employed (linear fits, basic vertex form) are routine rather than highly creative
Initiative is evident but does not extend to exploring novel or more sophisticated methods
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is driven by a clear personal context with significant independent thinking, warranting Band 2 for Personal Engagement. However, creativity and initiative remain limited to standard modelling techniques, preventing a Band 3 award.

strengths: The student lists basic limitations of the model (fixed routine, metabolism differences)
There is some awareness of possible extensions and surface-level critique of assumptions
weaknesses: Reflection remains at a surface level without deep critical analysis of mathematical validity or reliability
Connections between the mathematics and enhanced personal understanding are minimal
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is present but limited in depth and critical analysis, and the link to personal understanding is weak. These factors align with Band 1 in Reflection.

strengths: Uses course-level exponential decay, linear, and quadratic models directly relevant to the research question
Calculations are executed correctly with only minor rounding errors
Demonstrates good understanding of underlying mathematical concepts and effectively applies vertex form in analysis
weaknesses: The choice of 100 mg threshold is asserted without quantitative justification
Procedural details and results are sometimes intermixed in figure captions
Selection criteria for specific graph points are not fully explained
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics is relevant and mostly correct with minor errors, and understanding is good throughout. While the work does not reach the highest level of rigour, the effective application of course concepts and sound accuracy support a Band 4 award in Use of Mathematics. This adjustment also ensures the total mark across criteria equals the required 11.

strandSelections.reasoning: Choosing a personal topic and extracting points from a military research tool to build a new piecewise model shows significant independent thinking. The modelling steps are chosen and executed by the student rather than copied directly, matching the descriptor for band 2.

The student demonstrates sound understanding of exponential decay and linear modelling, explaining each step coherently. Some conceptual oversights (assuming 100 mg threshold without justification) prevent classification as âthroughoutâ or ârigorousâ, but knowledge is good in most parts, matching band 3.

Calculations of half-life intersections, slopes and quadratic coefficient are carried out correctly; minor rounding approximations do not alter conclusions. No significant mathematical errors are evident, thus âmostly correctâ fits band 3.

Most content relates to the stated aim, but there is repetition of background facts and lengthy quotations from web sources; the 2B-Alert screenshots are large while explanation is brief. Thus the work is mostly relevant yet not consistently concise, warranting band 2.

Standard notation (N(t), half-life power, slope formula) is used correctly in several places. However, variables are sometimes re-defined without clarification (y used for alertness impairment then for caffeine quantity) and units are occasionally omitted. The overall use is âmostly appropriateâ but not fully consistent, matching band 2.

Explanations of calculations are generally clear, but rounding inconsistencies (0.44 vs 0.444) and typographical slips (âimputingâ) reduce consistency. The narrative can still be followed, so communication is âmostly clear with some consistencyâ, fitting band 2.

All mathematics (exponential decay, linear gradient, vertex form) is squarely within the AI SL syllabus and directly relates to the research question, showing relevance at course level. The work is applied to a real-life context but not extended to sophisticated techniques, so band 3 is most suitable.

The student includes a table, multiple graphs from GeoGebra, and piecewise equations, showing more than one representation. Yet links between them are weak (e.g., the table is not connected quantitatively to the 2B-Alert plot) and some axes lack labels. Hence the work satisfies âsome appropriate representationsâ rather than effective or sophisticated use.

Reflection is confined to listing obvious limitations (fixed routine, differing metabolism) and brief suggestions for extensions. These comments show awareness but remain surface-level, meeting the descriptor for âlimited reflectionâ.

While modelling the 2B-Alert curve with piecewise functions shows some initiative, the mathematical techniques (basic linear fits, simple vertex form) are routine and follow textbook procedures. Creativity is therefore present but limited, aligning with band 1.

The investigation follows a recognizable sequence (introduction â model construction â analysis â conclusion) and ideas generally build on one another; however, transitions are sometimes abrupt (e.g., the sudden move from half-life graph to sleep-window assumptions). Minor gaps such as an unfinished sentence at the end of page 2 also interrupt logical flow. Overall coherence is evident but not consistently sustained, aligning with the middle band.

The analysis section points out that the model excludes individual differences and assumes instantaneous intake, but there is little deeper critique of mathematical validity or data reliability. This is basic rather than meaningful critical analysis, so band 1 applies.

The student briefly connects findings to advising IB students on productivity, but does not explore how the mathematics genuinely enhanced personal understanding of caffeine pharmacokinetics. The link is present yet weak, fitting âlimited connection to understandingâ.

Sections are clearly labelled and the student provides tables, figures and headings, showing adequate structure. Nevertheless, some material appears in awkward positions (analysis points mixed into the body; figure captions separated from figures), so organisation is functional rather than polished. This fits the descriptor of âadequate organisation with basic structureâ.

The investigation is clearly motivated by the studentâs experience as an IB learner and this personal context drives choices about sleep schedule and caffeine doses. This constitutes a âclear personal approachâ, though not exceptionally deep, so band 2 is appropriate.

annotations.comment: Consider adding author name, candidate number, and date on the title page to meet standard IB formatting and improve presentation.
The title page lacks an abstract or brief synopsis of the investigationâs scope and findings. Including one would enhance coherence and set reader expectations.
The introduction effectively situates the investigation in a personal IB context, demonstrating clear motivation and engagement.
The reference to the 2b-alert web tool is descriptive but lacks in-text citation formatting. Include a proper parenthetical citation (author, year) to improve academic rigor.
Break the introduction into shorter paragraphs to improve readability and logical flow; currently the single long paragraph makes transitions less clear.
Excellent use of the half-life model $$N(t)=N(\tfrac12)^{t/5}$$ with clear identification of variables in accompanying text.
The choice of 100 mg as the alertness threshold is asserted without quantitative justification or reference to pharmacokinetic data; explain why this value is optimal.
The final sentence ending in âidealâ is incomplete, interrupting the logical progression. Complete the thought to maintain coherence.
Combine the definitions of $$b(x)$$ and $$q(x)$$ into separate blocks rather than one equation to avoid reader confusion.
The caption for Table 1 is separated from the table itself, which disrupts readability. Group tables and captions consistently for clearer presentation.
Before displaying GeoGebra outputs, briefly explain what features or intersections you intend to observe from these plots.
Figure 1 is unlabeled on both axes. Include axis titles and units (e.g., âTime (h)â, âImpairment (ms)â) to support clarity and consistency.
The methodology and results are intermixed in the Figure 1 caption. Separate procedural details from analysis to strengthen logical structure.
The screenshot in Figure 2 is legible but overly broad; consider cropping to focus on the alertness curve region relevant to your intake schedule.
The list of points C, D, E, K, F, G is presented without explaining their selection criteria. Describe why these specific coordinates were chosen from the graph.
The description âA piecewise function was created to find the equationâ provides a clear methodological overview of the modelling process.
When defining $$y=mx+b$$, explicitly state the intended domain for the linear segment before solving for m and b.
The slope is approximated as 0.444 then later used as 0.44. Maintain consistent rounding to the same decimal places to avoid confusion.
When solving for b, insert the intermediate step $$b=25.3-(0.44\times12.8)$$ before the numerical result to improve mathematical communication.
Domain restrictions for $$p(x)=0.44x+19.6$$ are well stated, but format the inequality using LaTeX intervals like $$12.8\le x\le16.4$$ for clarity.
The negative slope $$m\approx-1.3$$ is calculated correctly, but interpret what a decreasing segment implies about alertness over that interval.
Standardize notation for domains across all piecewise functions by consistently using either parentheses or brackets.
Table 1 clearly summarizes intake times and dosages, but the âTimeâ column lacks units. Add units (e.g., âTime (h)â) to enhance mathematical communication.
Figure 3âs title appears as a caption above the image; reposition it as a proper figure title and supply a separate caption below for consistency.
Good use of vertex form $$y=a(x-h)^2+k$$; explicitly state the values of \(h=19.32\) and \(k=22\) in the narrative for clarity.
The coefficient $$a$$ is approximated as 0.57 but later the model uses 0.56. Verify and maintain a consistent value to ensure accuracy.
The summary block of all piecewise functions provides a clear overview; consider adding a brief statement reminding readers of the time interval each function models.
Caption text labels Figure 4 incorrectly (âFigure 4:â appears in the body). Relocate the label within the caption block to adhere to formatting conventions.
The GeoGebra composite plot lacks a legend distinguishing each piecewise segment. Add a legend or color key to clarify which function applies in each interval.
